checklist: Review every release-blocker fixture under fixtures/platform-release-blockers/.
Confirm at least one maintainer can reproduce each support category without private user data.
Close only with a verified command, documented platform limitation, or merged docs/code fix.

labels.close_when: The linked verification command passes or the issue is documented as a platform limitation.
labels.label: intake
labels.meaning: New external adopter issue needs support bundle completeness check.

labels.close_when: The linked verification command passes or the issue is documented as a platform limitation.
labels.label: needs-repro

labels.close_when: The linked verification command passes or the issue is documented as a platform limitation.
labels.label: confirmed
labels.meaning: Maintainer reproduced the issue with current generated assets.

labels.close_when: The linked verification command passes or the issue is documented as a platform limitation.
labels.label: blocked-by-platform
labels.meaning: A host platform capability or localhost policy blocks the path.

labels.close_when: The linked verification command passes or the issue is documented as a platform limitation.
labels.label: docs-fix
labels.meaning: Documentation or copyable command fix is enough to close.

labels.close_when: The linked verification command passes or the issue is documented as a platform limitation.
labels.label: release-blocker
labels.meaning: A supported happy path fails with current release assets.

labels.close_when: The linked verification command passes or the issue is documented as a platform limitation.
labels.label: resolved
labels.meaning: Reporter or maintainer verified the closing command.

purpose: Turn support desk triage into a first-adopter onboarding path and maintainer SLA loop that can be verified before an alpha release is promoted.

failure_fallback_path: Attach only the redacted platform-support-bundle-v1 fields to the matching GitHub issue template.
shortest_success_path: Start Study Anything with Skill Mode or the published image.
Import the generated OpenAPI or OpenAI-compatible tools from the adoption pack.
Ask the platform Agent to create one session, add one cited reading, run quiz generation, answer once, and check mastery.
